payload.answer: Treat this as a temporal status update for the same project. The prior state (planned/open) is superseded by the newer state that the MVP is implemented; do not retain the old status as current.
citations: Existing: "The Brain Slack agent is planned, status=open"
New: "The Brain Slack agent MVP is now implemented"

entity_resolution.reason: "The Brain Slack agent" and "The Brain Slack agent MVP" refer to the same underlying project, with the new text indicating a more specific implementation stage rather than a distinct entity.

memory_cards.statement: The Brain Slack agent has transitioned from planned/open to implemented at MVP level.

details: Detected same project despite punctuation/phrasing variation.
Classified change as a temporal progression in status.
Recommended preserving older status only as historical, not current.

repair_options: Update the existing project record status from open/planned to implemented_at=MVP.
Store the prior planned/open state only as historical context, not as the current status.
Normalize punctuation and naming so "The Brain Slack agent" and "The Brain Slack agent MVP" resolve to one project entity.
